question_text: A square has side length 6 cm. What is the area of one corner triangle if the octagon is formed by joining midpoints?

steps.description: Find the legs of the corner triangle
steps.calculation: Each leg is half the square side: 6/2 = 3 cm

diagram_requirements.diagram_specification: Show square with side 6, highlight one corner triangle with legs of 3 cm

mistake_patterns.pattern: Using full side length instead of half
mistake_patterns.why_wrong: Corner triangle legs are half the square side, not full side
Triangle area = (1/2) × base × height, not base × height
